summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal satisfied (no new tables, functions within existing lcs schema which has canonical+error from Phase 2A), Registry-First (N/A — no tables created), Sovereign ID (no CL identity minting, CID/SID/MID lifecycle within LCS), No sideways calls (reads outreach.outreach + cl.company_identity for FK validation, writes only within lcs schema). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — 7/8 acceptance criteria fully met, 1 implicit. fn_promote_emission(uuid) with atomic single-row flow: FOR UPDATE lock → validate STAGED → INSERT cid_intake → fn_process_cid handoff → PROMOTED. Error paths: log lcs_errors → UPDATE status REJECTED/ERROR → RETURN clean (correctness patch confirmed — no RAISE after state mutation). fn_promote_emission_batch(int) with deterministic ORDER BY created_at, LIMIT, return-state logic (reads status after each call, no exception control flow). cid_intake insert restriction is implicit through function encapsulation (no explicit role). OBSERVATIONS: (1) CRITICAL correctness patch confirmed — builder resolved RAISE-after-DML from original SRC3. All error paths now RETURN clean. CS documents correctness_patch_applied=true. (2) Three schema adaptations documented: ingest_status→status, emitted_at→created_at, reject_reason→error_id FK linkage. (3) Movement type registry validation skipped at promotion time — defensible since fn_emit_movement validates at emission. (4) reject_reason column not populated inline — function uses error_id FK to lcs_errors (single source of truth, architecturally sound but differs from WP literal wording). (5) No explicit promotion role created (unlike Phase 2A lcs_emitter and Phase 2B identity_resolver). Phase 2C satisfied by SRC3 post-correctness-patch.
